AQA.name: Paper 1
AQA.title: Atomic structure and the periodic table, Bonding, structure and properties, Quantitative chemistry, Chemical changes, Energy changes

AQA.name: Paper 2
AQA.title: Rate and extent of chemical change, Organic chemistry, Chemical analysis, Chemistry of the atmosphere, Using resources

Edexcel.name: Paper 1
Edexcel.title: Key concepts in chemistry, States of matter, Methods of separating and purifying, The Periodic Table, Chemical changes, Acids, Electrolytic processes, Obtaining and using metals, Reversible reactions

Edexcel.name: Paper 2
Edexcel.title: Key concepts in chemistry, Groups of the Periodic Table, Rates of reaction, Fuels, Heat energy changes in chemical reactions, Earth and atmospheric science, Qualitative analysis, Quantitative analysis

OCR.name: Paper 1
OCR.title: Particles, Chemical reactions, Elements, compounds and mixtures, The Periodic Table, Chemical bonds, Structure and properties

OCR.name: Paper 2
OCR.title: Particles, Chemical reactions, Elements, compounds and mixtures, The Periodic Table, Chemical bonds, Structure and properties, Quantitative chemistry, Global challenges, Chemical analysis

CCEA.name: Paper 1
CCEA.title: Structures, reactions and uses of chemicals, Earth and its atmosphere

CCEA.name: Paper 2
CCEA.title: Structures, reactions and uses of chemicals, Earth and its atmosphere, Quantitative chemistry

assessmentObjectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures
Apply knowledge and understanding of scientific ideas, techniques and procedures
Analyse information and ideas to interpret and evaluate scientific ideas and processes

strands.name: Atomic Structure and the Periodic Table

topics.name: Atoms and Elements
topics.description: Atoms, elements, compounds, mixtures and chemical equations

topics.name: The Periodic Table
topics.description: Development and structure of the Periodic Table, properties of groups

topics.name: History of the Atom
topics.description: Development of the atomic model from Dalton to Bohr

topics.name: Mixtures and Separation Techniques
topics.description: Methods of separating mixtures including distillation, chromatography and crystallisation

topics.name: Ionic Bonding
topics.description: Ionic bonding, structure and properties of ionic compounds

topics.name: Covalent Bonding
topics.description: Covalent bonding, simple molecules and giant covalent structures

topics.name: Metallic Bonding and Alloys
topics.description: Metallic bonding, properties of metals and alloys

topics.name: States of Matter
topics.description: States of matter and state changes with particle theory

topics.name: Nanoscience and Nanoparticles
topics.description: Nanoparticles, their properties and uses

topics.name: Relative Masses and Moles
topics.description: Relative atomic mass, relative formula mass and the mole concept

topics.name: Reacting Masses
topics.description: Calculating masses of reactants and products from equations

topics.name: Concentration and Yield
topics.description: Concentration of solutions, percentage yield and atom economy

topics.name: Gas Volumes
topics.description: Volume of gases and the ideal gas equation

topics.name: Titrations
topics.description: Titration technique, calculations and concordant results

topics.name: Reactivity of Metals
topics.description: The reactivity series, displacement reactions and metal extraction

topics.name: Reactions of Acids
topics.description: Acids, bases, alkalis, neutralisation and making salts

topics.name: Electrolysis
topics.description: Electrolysis of molten and aqueous ionic compounds

topics.name: Oxidation and Reduction
topics.description: Oxidation and reduction in terms of electrons, half equations and ionic equations

topics.name: Exothermic and Endothermic Reactions
topics.description: Energy changes in reactions, reaction profiles and activation energy

topics.name: Chemical Cells and Fuel Cells
topics.description: How chemical cells and hydrogen fuel cells produce electricity

strands.name: Rate and Extent of Chemical Change

topics.name: Rate of Reaction
topics.description: Factors affecting rate and measuring rate of reaction using collision theory

topics.name: Reversible Reactions and Equilibrium
topics.description: Reversible reactions, dynamic equilibrium and Le Chatelier's principle

topics.name: Hydrocarbons and Crude Oil
topics.description: Crude oil, fractional distillation and properties of hydrocarbons

topics.name: Cracking and Alkenes
topics.description: Cracking of hydrocarbons and properties of alkenes

topics.name: Alcohols, Carboxylic Acids and Esters
topics.description: Functional groups and reactions of alcohols, carboxylic acids and esters

topics.name: Polymers
topics.description: Addition and condensation polymers, their formation and uses

topics.name: Pure Substances and Formulations
topics.description: Purity, formulations and identification of substances using melting and boiling points

topics.name: Chromatography
topics.description: Paper chromatography, Rf values and interpreting chromatograms

topics.name: Testing for Ions and Gases
topics.description: Chemical tests for cations, anions and gases

topics.name: Earth's Early Atmosphere
topics.description: The evolution of the Earth's atmosphere over billions of years

topics.name: Greenhouse Gases and Climate Change
topics.description: Greenhouse gases, human activities and their effect on climate

topics.name: Air Pollution
topics.description: Atmospheric pollutants and their effects on health and the environment

topics.name: Potable Water
topics.description: Potable water, water treatment and desalination

topics.name: Waste Water Treatment
topics.description: Sewage treatment and processing waste water

topics.name: Corrosion and Its Prevention
topics.description: Rusting of iron and methods of corrosion prevention

topics.name: Alloys, Ceramics and Composites
topics.description: Properties and uses of alloys, ceramics, polymers and composites

topics.name: Life Cycle Assessment and Recycling
topics.description: LCA, sustainability, recycling and reducing resource use

topics.name: Using Materials
topics.description: The Haber process, NPK fertilisers and industrial processes

notes.tripleVsCombined: Triple Chemistry includes all Combined Science Chemistry content in greater depth, plus additional topics. Topics unique to Triple or significantly expanded include: separation techniques, nanoscience, titrations, gas volumes, oxidation and reduction (half equations), alcohols/carboxylic acids/esters in more detail, polymers (addition and condensation), chromatography detail, waste water treatment, corrosion prevention, alloys/ceramics/composites, life cycle assessment detail, the Haber process and NPK fertilisers.
notes.requiredPracticals: AQA has 8 required practicals: preparing a salt, electrolysis, energy changes in reactions, investigating reaction rates, chromatography, identifying ions, water purification, and temperature changes.
notes.mathsSkills: Chemistry requires maths skills including: calculating relative formula mass, moles, reacting masses, concentration, percentage yield, atom economy, gas volumes, interpreting graphs, drawing tangents to curves, calculating gradients, using standard form and significant figures.
notes.workingScientifically: All exam boards assess Working Scientifically: experimental design, variables, risk assessment, data analysis, evaluating methods, drawing conclusions, identifying sources of error.
